codeReview.summary: The patch fixes the non-generic wildcard cases from the prior review and removes the bare-vs-instantiated nominal shortcut, but it does not fully resolve the original soundness defect: in-scope generic parameters are still treated as wildcards, so generic bodies and generic callers can still mix unrelated type parameters or pass a generic value where a concrete type is required.

issues.description: `types_compatible` still accepts any `TParam` pair (`line 191`) and any in-scope `TParam` against arbitrary types (`lines 192-193`). Because `expect_type` uses the current generic scope (`lines 205-206`) and `check_fn_args` unions the callee and caller generic names before calling the same predicate (`lines 409-415`), programs in generic scopes remain unsound: e.g. `fn bad<A, B>(a: A, b: B) -> A { b }`, `let x: A = 42`, or `fn foo<T>(x: T) { takes_i32(x) }` would still typecheck. That is the core defect the original failed review described, so this rerun fix is incomplete.

summary: Reviewed the original failed review, mission metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, compiler-worker skill, commit `1323fae324a6dfb5a096595c9a5c570e183a9b57`, and the current `lib/typecheck.ml`/`test/test_typecheck.ml` state. The fix repairs the specific non-generic regressions covered by the new tests, but the original soundness problem is still present inside generic scopes because in-scope type parameters remain wildcards, so this review stays in fail status.
